channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Graham Platner makes it official in Maine, submitting paperwork to leave Senate race
item.description: In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.
item.content:encoded:
In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.

(Image credit: CJ Gunther)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Patriotic art gets the spotlight as NEA funding shifts. Cue 'The Ronald Reagan Overture'
item.description: Patriotic art and music is taking center stage this year under the Trump Administration, as funds shift away from DEI. For some orgs, like the Reagan Presidential Library, this is their wheelhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Patriotic art and music is taking center stage this year under the Trump Administration, as funds shift away from DEI. For some orgs, like the Reagan Presidential Library, this is their wheelhouse.

(Image credit: Consolidated News Pictures/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Houston neighbors started seeing more ICE agents around. Then came a fatal shooting.
item.description: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was a 52-year-old Mexican national who worked in construction for more than three decades. The father of three was shot by Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents after they attempted to pull him over.
item.content:encoded:
Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was a 52-year-old Mexican national who worked in construction for more than three decades. The father of three was shot by Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents after they attempted to pull him over.

(Image credit: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán
)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Colin Farrell plays a P.I. with the strangest of secrets in 'Sugar' Season 2
item.description: The Apple TV series wraps noir inside science fiction. With subtlety and charm, Farrell plays an earnest alien just doing his best as a private eye in Los Angeles.
item.content:encoded: The Apple TV series wraps noir inside science fiction. With subtlety and charm, Farrell plays an earnest alien just doing his best as a private eye in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: New 'Little House' remake will inspire you to rewatch the '70s TV series
item.description: Netflix's new Little House series features the same characters and setting as the original, but its reliance on hand-held cameras, in extreme close-up, calls too much attention to itself.
item.content:encoded:
Netflix's new Little House series features the same characters and setting as the original, but its reliance on hand-held cameras, in extreme close-up, calls too much attention to itself.

(Image credit: Eric Zachanowich)
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: Largest housing affordability bill in decades becomes law without Trump's signature
item.description: President Trump refused to sign the bill without Congress first passing his sweeping voter ID bill.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump refused to sign the bill without Congress first passing his sweeping voter ID bill.

(Image credit: Francisco Seco)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: These three artists are poised to invade the top of the pop charts
item.description: We're in that phase of summer pop doldrums when the same songs seem to be on repeat week after week. Can Stella Lefty, Yung Miami or Malcolm Todd make a run to crack the top 10?
item.content:encoded:
We're in that phase of summer pop doldrums when the same songs seem to be on repeat week after week. Can Stella Lefty, Yung Miami or Malcolm Todd make a run to crack the top 10?

(Image credit: Halle-Jean March)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Thompson

item.title: Taliban declares war on smartphones
item.description: A newly announced ban on smartphones for government workers, police and military personnel is spilling over into healthcare and educational facilities. Ordinary citizens worry they'll be next.
item.content:encoded:
A newly announced ban on smartphones for government workers, police and military personnel is spilling over into healthcare and educational facilities. Ordinary citizens worry they'll be next.

(Image credit: Wakil Kohsar/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Faizi

item.title: Baby Rose's voice is always searching. 'YEARNALISM' gives it a home
item.description: The singular singer's third album finds a message worthy of her instrument: Great romances are defined not by what you want, but by the act of wanting.
item.content:encoded:
The singular singer's third album finds a message worthy of her instrument: Great romances are defined not by what you want, but by the act of wanting.

(Image credit: Louisa Meng)
item.dc:creator: Sheldon Pearce

item.title: Waymo called the cops on teen riders, raising privacy concerns
item.description: Two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from a driverless taxi when the company disabled it and alerted police.
item.content:encoded:
Two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from a driverless taxi when the company disabled it and alerted police.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman
